initiatives.name: Slingshot Collective
initiatives.description: Berkeley-based quarterly, independent, radical, newspaper published since 1988.

initiatives.name: Yalla Berkeley
initiatives.description: Info on how to mobilize the city of Berkeley to support Palestine

initiatives.name: The Long Haul
initiatives.description: A radical space in south Berkeley with a lending library, zine archive, meeting room, and work spaces for community groups and DIY projects that hosts events.

initiatives.name: La Peña Cultural Center
initiatives.description: A center for Latinx, Caribbean, and Indigenous diasporic communities of the Bay Area to preserve and celebrate cultural traditions, present new interdisciplinary creative works, and to nurture grassroots social justice movements with artists, activists, and allies.

initiatives.name: Revolution Books
initiatives.description: A radical bookstore fostering mutual aid and critical consciousness through literature, political theory, and community events. They coordinate book talks, study groups, and solidarity initiatives while building power through revolutionary education, cultural programming, and creating spaces for radical thought and collective learning.

initiatives.name: Berkeley Free Clinic
initiatives.description: A volunteer-run free clinic fostering mutual aid and solidarity through accessible healthcare since 1969. They coordinate medical services, dental care, and peer counseling while building power through community health education, harm reduction, and creating spaces for equitable healthcare access regardless of ability to pay.

initiatives.name: Berkeley Copwatch
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community safety through police monitoring and accountability since 1990. They coordinate community patrols, know-your-rights trainings, and documentation of police conduct while building power through direct action, public education, and creating networks of community-based oversight and support for those impacted by police violence.

initiatives.name: Where Do We Go Berkeley?
initiatives.description: Where Do We Go Berkeley is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded by Berkeleys unhoused residents offering advocacy and material support people on the street.

initiatives.name: People's Park Berkeley
initiatives.description: National Historic Landmark on Ohlone land Liberated for the people on 4/20/1969 Raided by @ucberkeleyofficial on 1/4/2024
